title: Ninra Narayana Perumal temple
tagline: Ninra Narayana Perumal temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: Ninra Narayana Perumal temple, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Ninra Narayana Perumal Temple (also called Nindra Narayana Perumal Temple) in Thiruthangal, a neighborhood of Sivakasi City Municipal Corporation in the South Indian state of Tamil
overview.description: Ninra Narayana Perumal Temple (also called Nindra Narayana Perumal Temple) in Thiruthangal, a neighborhood of Sivakasi City Municipal Corporation in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Divya Prabandha, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries AD. It is one of the 108 Divya Desam dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Ninra Narayana and his consort Lakshmi as Arunakamala Mahadevi. The temple in its present form was believed to have be

topPlaces.name: Karunellinathar temple
topPlaces.description: Karunellinathar temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva in Thiruthangal, a town in the outskirts of Sivakasi, in Tamil Nadu, India. Shiva is worshiped as Karunellinathar, and is represented by the lingam and his consort Parvati is depicted as Chokki Amman.

topPlaces.name: Pallapatti, Virudhunagar
topPlaces.description: Pallapatti is a panchayat town in Virudhunagar district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Thiruthangal
topPlaces.description: Thiruthangal is a neighbourhood of Sivakasi City, India.Thiruthangal is one of the Zones of Sivakasi City Municipal Corporation. Ninra Narayana Perumal temple, one among the 108 Divya Desams, Vishnu temples revered by saint poets, Alwars of 6th–9th century is located in Thiruthangal.

topPlaces.name: Kakkivadanpatti
topPlaces.description: Kakkivadanpatti is a small village in Virudhunagar district, Tamil Nadu, India, near Sivakasi. Most of the population are farmers and speak both Tamil and Telugu.

topPlaces.name: Alamarathupatti
topPlaces.description: Alamarathupatti is a village and panchayat in Sivakasi block, Virudhunagar district, Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Anaikuttam Dam
topPlaces.description: Anaikuttam Dam is a dam situated in Tamil Nadu's Virudhunagar district. The dam is built on the Arjuna River.

topPlaces.name: Badrakali Amman temple, Sivakasi
topPlaces.description: Badrakali Amman Temple in Sivakasi, a town in Virudhunagar district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Badrakali. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is believed to have been built during the 18th century with later expansion during the 19th and 20th centuries.

topPlaces.name: Sivakasi
topPlaces.description: Sivakasi (also pronounced Shivakaashi) is a city in Virudhunagar District in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The city is known for firecrackers and match factories that produce 70% of the country's output.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Badrakali Amman temple, Sivakasi & around

items.note: Badrakali Amman Temple in Sivakasi, a town in Virudhunagar district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Badrakali. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is believed to have been built during the 18th century with later expansion during the 19th and 20th centuries.

Sivakasi (also pronounced Shivakaashi) is a city in Virudhunagar District in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The city is known for firecrackers and match factories that produce 70% of the country's output.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ninra Narayana Perumal temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ninra Narayana Perumal temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ninra Narayana Perumal temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (50 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (59 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ninra Narayana Perumal temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ninra Narayana Perumal temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ninra Narayana Perumal temple?
faq.a: Stays in Ninra Narayana Perumal temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ninra Narayana Perumal temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Karunellinathar temple, Pallapatti, Virudhunagar, Thiruthangal, Kakkivadanpatti, Alamarathupatti.
